channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Contract for Hong Kong company to operate Panama Canal ports had irregularities, audit finds
item.description: Panama’s comptroller authority says an audit found irregularities in the renewal of a 25-year port concession in the interoceanic canal and it would request an investigation into the authorization of the contract to a Hong Kong company.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Altar found in Guatemalan jungle evidence of mingling of Mayan and Teotihuacan cultures, experts say
item.description: An altar from the Teotihuacan culture, at the pre-Hispanic heart of what became Mexico, has been discovered in Tikal National Park in Guatemala, the center of Mayan culture, demonstrating the interaction between the two societies.
item.dc:creator: Sonia Pérez D., Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dutch cannabis growers welcome expansion of state-run experiment to make sales legal
item.description: BEMMEL, Netherlands (AP) — The Netherlands expanded a government-run initiative on Monday allowing legal cannabis sales. While growing cannabis is still illegal, cannabis shops — known as coffeeshops — in 10 municipalities will be allowed to sell marijuana from 10 licensed producers.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Duke basketball star could be deported under Trump visa policy
item.description: The State Department announced Saturday that visas of South Sudanese passport holders would be revoked.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Rescue efforts from Myanmar's deadly earthquake wind down as death toll hits 3,600
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Long-shot efforts to find survivors from Myanmar’s devastating March 28 earthquake were winding down Monday, as rescue efforts were supplanted by increasing relief and recovery activity. The death toll surpassed 3,600 and was still climbing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Spartz says Ukraine not positioned to keep land in peace talks
item.description: Rep. Victoria Spartz (R-Ind.) said in a new interview that Ukraine is not positioned to keep some of its land in peace talks with Russia. “I just don’t see how they [Ukraine] can be positioned to demand to keep the land.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he’s not looking at a pause on tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday said he’s not considering a pause on tariffs, following an unconfirmed report earlier in the day that he was considering a 90-day pause, which caused the stock market to jump. “We’re not looking at that,” Trump said when asked if he’s open to a pause.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Bessent opening trade negotiations with Japan
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced Monday the U.S. is opening trade negotiations with Japan. Bessent, in a post on the social platform X, said President Trump has tasked him and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer to lead the talks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: UN mission details deadly attacks in Haiti's capital and questions government's delayed response
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — More than 260 people were killed during recent gang attacks on two communities in Haiti’s capital, according to a report on Monday by the U.N. political mission in Haiti that questioned the delay in the response by authorities.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani security forces kill 9 militants in a raid near the Afghan border, the military says
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani security forces raided a militant hideout in the restive northwest near the Afghan border, killing nine militants, the military said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7 GOP senators sign on to bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators, including Sen. Chuck Grassley (Iowa), the Senate’s president pro tempore, and Sen. Mitch McConnell (Ky.), the former Senate Republican leader, have signed on to a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Zen the border collie teaches other dogs how to rescue people in Italy's avalanche-prone Dolomites
item.description: CORTINA D'AMPEZZO, Italy (AP) — Zen, a 5-year-old border collie, circled friskily around a mound of snow as he picked up a scent, his quick movements signaling to his handler that someone was buried deep below.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and ANDREA ROSA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Trade deficit with China must be resolved before deal on tariffs
item.description: President Trump said the trade deficit between the U.S. and China must be resolved before he is willing to negotiate a deal the regarding tariffs he placed on Beijing. “When you look at the trade deficit we have with certain countries … with China, it’s a trillion dollars.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas terror outlet quietly cuts Gaza death count, reveals most killed were combat-age men
item.description: New analysis shows thousands of names were removed from the Gaza casualty list as Hamas terror organization admits most deaths aged between 13 to 55 were males.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Cambodia's leader presides at ceremony for upgrade of naval base with help from China
item.description: SIHANOUKVILLE, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet on Saturday presided over the opening of an expansion of his country’s main naval base, which analysts and the U.S. government suspect will be used as a strategic outpost by China.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India's parliament passes bill that would change Muslim land endowments
item.description: India's parliament passes a bill amending Muslim land endowments, sparking protests over concerns it undermines minority rights and could lead to property confiscation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luxury cruise ship caught in 40-foot waves while traveling through Drake Passage
item.description: Passengers on Quark Expeditions' Ocean Explorer ship got rocked with waves up to 40 feet on a recent cruise, rocking those onboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The official said that President Trump has “recently threatened to impose strong sanctions on Russia” to further explain leaving out Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: “Tragically, three soldiers were found deceased in Lithuania yesterday, and it pains me to confirm that today the fourth soldier was also found deceased,” White House said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
